hero.eyebrow: CRM · ERP · Business Intelligence — one platform
hero.body: Corex360 brings sales, operations and finance into one living data layer. No more scattered spreadsheets — just a real-time, AI-powered, 360° view.

intro.label: What is Corex360
intro.title: Not scattered tools — one layer of truth for your business.
intro.body: Corex360 unifies CRM, ERP and business intelligence with every process — WhatsApp, Instagram, your website, call center, fleet tracking and document filing — in one live source of data. Every customer touch, every vehicle in the field and every number on one screen; managers see the whole business from one place, and knowledge stays in the company even when people leave.

about.label: About
about.title: We build the software that runs a business in 360° — from Türkiye.
about.lead: Corex360 is an integrated business management platform that unifies a company's scattered processes in a single digital hub. From sales to operations, customer relations to reporting, it makes every workflow more visible, measurable and manageable.

story.label: Our story
story.title: Born from a real need.
story.p1: We saw growing businesses lose efficiency between disparate software, disconnected spreadsheets and scattered communication channels. We believed every process — from sales to operations, customer relations to reporting — should be managed from a single hub.
story.p2: Corex360 was born from that need: an integrated management platform that unifies CRM, ERP, business intelligence and communication channels in one live data layer, adapting to a team's daily workflow.

group.label: Corporate structure
group.title: An affiliate of SAVAŞ GRUP.
group.body: Corex360 is the technology affiliate of the long-established SAVAŞ GRUP SANAYİ ve TİCARET A.Ş. It was built by combining the group's experience in industry, trade and field operations with modern software engineering.
group.body2: That's why Corex360 is more than a software product; it's an integrated management platform born from real business needs, shaped from within operations and built to fit the daily processes of growing companies.

team.title: A team from the field
team.sub: We combine our expertise from different disciplines with a product vision that understands real business processes.
team.careersSub: Want to walk toward the same vision with us?

items.desc: Our product comes from the daily operations of real businesses, not from theory.

items.title: Simplicity
items.desc: We deliver enterprise power in a simple experience every team can use with ease.

items.title: Respect for data
items.desc: Your data is yours — protected securely, transparently and GDPR/KVKK compliant.

hero.titlePre: Every business has its own order.
hero.lead: From logistics to healthcare, from retail to education, every sector has its own rhythm. Corex360 unites sales, operations, customer relations and reporting in a single live data layer — without breaking that rhythm.

approach.label: Our approach
approach.title: Not separate software per sector, but one platform that adapts to each.
approach.p1: Most businesses buy a separate tool for every need: one for stock, one for the field, one for accounting. Over time these tools turn into disconnected islands, and the real cost shows up not in licences but in lost data and repeated work.
approach.p2: Corex360 does the opposite. It brings CRM, ERP and business intelligence together in one core, configured to your sector's language, your teams' habits and the reality on the ground.
approach.coreLabel: One core, configured to every sector's language.
approach.quote: You fit the software to your business, not your business to the software.

sectors.q: Which sectors is Corex360 suited to?
sectors.a: It adapts to any business whose processes are driven by data — logistics, construction, hospitality, healthcare, retail and education above all. The core is sector-agnostic; the flows are configured for you.

notListed.q: I can't see my sector in the list — can I still use it?
notListed.a: Yes. The listed sectors are the ones we work with most; the product is designed to fit any operation. Share your needs and we'll put together a configuration tailored to you.

adapt.q: How quickly can it be adapted to my sector?
adapt.a: Ready-made sector flows go live in minutes; configuring custom processes usually takes a few days. For complex integrations, our team supports you end to end.

data.a: Your data is yours — stored encrypted, isolated and compliant with KVKK and GDPR. Access rights are role-based and every action is logged.

migrate.q: Can I migrate my existing data?
migrate.a: Data import is supported via Excel, existing CRM/ERP systems and API. Our team plans the migration so your operation keeps running without interruption.

hero.lead: Real experiences from teams that put Corex360 at the centre of their business — the move from scattered to in order, in their own words.

care.label: Customers first
care.title: Every story starts with a relationship of trust.
care.p1: To us a customer isn't a closed deal but a path walked together. We're beside you at setup, hold your hand through the first weeks, and scale with you as you grow.
care.p2: Because Corex360 was born in the field; we know what it takes to keep a business running. That's why every piece of feedback shapes the roadmap, and every support request reaches a real person.

logistics.author: Operations Manager
logistics.quote: We chased vehicles, drivers and trips across separate spreadsheets. Corex360 brought it all onto one screen; now the first thing we do each morning is check the panel.

retail.author: Retail Director
retail.quote: Managing every branch separately slowed us down. Uniting them in one panel cut shrinkage and let us decide far faster.

construction.author: Project Coordinator
construction.quote: The site always reached the office with a delay. Now we see progress live; solving an issue the same day feels completely different.

learn.p2: With short, clear, step-by-step videos we guide you from setup to advanced features. The goal: from the very first week, your team reaches for the panel on its own.

hero.lead: Behind the scenes of running a business from a single hub: notes on vision, product, engineering, and the field. Not marketing lines, but real operations.

tek-cati-altinda-360.title: 360° under one roof: the story of closing eight apps
tek-cati-altinda-360.excerpt: WhatsApp, CRM, inventory, invoicing, vehicle tracking, and filing no longer live in separate windows; how does a business change when they all meet in a single command center?

blocks.text: A business owner's morning often starts like this: a customer waiting on WhatsApp Web, an unanswered DM on Instagram, yesterday's invoice sitting in a separate accounting program, an inventory count on another screen, and a field supervisor wondering where the vehicle is. Eight different apps, eight different passwords, and eight data islands that know nothing about one another. Corex360 was built precisely against this scatter: instead of splitting a business into eight pieces, you run it from a single 360° command center.
The real cost of app inflation

Every new tool costs far more than another subscription invoice. The real price is the fragmentation of data. The customer writes on WhatsApp, the sales team logs them in an Excel sheet, the invoice is issued in another program, and the shipment is tracked in a third place. Nowhere does that customer's full story exist. When a question comes up — 'How much have we sold to this customer, what did we last discuss, has their payment come in?' — the answer costs three people, three screens, and half an hour.

Loss of context is a silent leak. The lead that slips between channels, the invoice issued twice, the product that shows in stock but isn't in the warehouse, the work order with no clear vehicle assignment. None of these is a crisis on its own; but when they repeat every single day, this invisible friction is what truly sets the pace of the business.
The problem isn't the number of apps, but that no single app ever sees the whole business.
What a single source of truth changes

In Corex360, every message from WhatsApp, Instagram, and the web form lands in the same inbox; every contact automatically becomes a CRM record and moves forward as an opportunity in the sales pipeline. Under that same customer card, the conversation history, the issued invoices, the products that left stock, and — if it's field work — the route of the vehicle dispatched to them all sit side by side. When data lives in one place, so does the context.

This convergence isn't just comfort; it's decision quality. When inventory and sales sit under one roof, you see which product really moves; when finance and CRM come together, which customer is profitable; when vehicle tracking and work orders combine, which field team works efficiently — all on a single BI dashboard. You don't try to merge eight reports; the report is born unified.
360° shouldn't be a luxury, but the default

Running a business shouldn't be the art of keeping eight tabs in sync. Corex360's vision is simple: communication, sales, production, logistics, finance, and documents — all in a single system that speaks the same language. When you stop switching windows, you win back time for your actual job: growing the business.

A command center is more than the sum of eight apps, because it makes them talk to one another. The whole story of Corex360 is hidden in that sentence: instead of managing the scatter, eliminating it.

omnichannel-gelen-kutusu.title: Every message is an opportunity: how the omnichannel inbox turns into sales
omnichannel-gelen-kutusu.excerpt: Every conversation from WhatsApp, Instagram DM, and the web form gathers in a single inbox, becomes a CRM contact, and moves through the sales pipeline without getting lost.

blocks.text: A customer writes once on WhatsApp, once on Instagram, and once through the form on the website. In most businesses, three channels are three separate worlds: one on the phone, one with the social media team, and one in an email inbox nobody opens. The result is familiar — the question left unanswered, the request that comes at night and is forgotten by morning, the mess of two people giving the same customer different answers. The omnichannel inbox reduces these three worlds to a single screen.
One inbox, one field of view

In Corex360, a WhatsApp message, an Instagram DM, and a web form drop into the same stream. A badge tells you which channel it came from; but you don't switch windows to reply. The team sees the same conversation, it's clear who has taken it on, and handoff happens in one click. The question 'Who looked at this message?' disappears, because everyone is looking at the same picture.

What matters is that speed becomes visible. Who responds and how quickly, which channel brings the most questions, which conversation turns into an opportunity — all of it is measured in the same place. The inbox stops being a pile and becomes the mouth of a sales pipeline.
A conversation's value shows not in which channel it came from, but in where it goes.
From message to CRM contact, from contact to opportunity

The real transformation happens here: every incoming contact automatically becomes a CRM record. That first 'Hi, can I get a price?' message turns into a record with a name, a channel, and a history. This contact opens as an opportunity in the sales pipeline; from first touch to quote, from quote to close, every stage lives on their card. No lead falls into the gap between channels.

You reply directly from the customer card. In front of you is not just a chat bubble; you have that customer's entire history, their past orders, their open opportunities, and even — once integrated — their invoices. As you write your reply, you know who they are, what they've bought before, and what stage they're at. You're not running generic support, but a sales conversation with context.
The lead that doesn't get lost, the sale that closes

Most sales are lost not because of a bad product, but because they weren't followed up. That is the quiet promise of the omnichannel inbox: no conversation is left half-finished, no opportunity is left unowned, no customer walks off to a competitor thinking 'I guess nobody replied.' What turns a message into a sale isn't magic; it's every touchpoint moving forward in one place, on one pipeline, with one clear owner.

When the wall between the social media team and the sales team comes down, a like from Instagram and a question from WhatsApp are managed with the same discipline. Marketing traffic genuinely starts turning into revenue — because now you know where that traffic goes.

multi-tenant-mimari.title: Thousands of businesses, one core: scaling a multi-tenant architecture
multi-tenant-mimari.excerpt: Data isolation, a single core serving thousands of tenants, event-driven real-time synchronization, and per-tenant KVKK compliance — from a production point of view.

blocks.text: A multi-tenant SaaS looks like a single application from the outside; on the inside, it's the discipline of carrying thousands of businesses' data on the same core without ever letting it touch. This is also the perennial topic of Corex360's monthly engineering webinar: how to build a system with a single codebase and a single infrastructure that still feels, to each tenant, like a world of its own.
Isolation: schema or row?

The first and most critical decision is data isolation. There are two main approaches: a separate schema for each tenant (schema-per-tenant), or tagging every row with a tenant_id in shared tables (row-level). The schema-based approach gives strong physical separation and easy backups, but managing tens of thousands of schemas makes migrations heavy. The row-based approach is operationally lightweight and scales; but a single missing WHERE clause is a disaster. In practice the hybrid wins: row-based isolation for most modules + row-level security policies (RLS) enforced at the database level, and logical separation for large enterprise tenants.

What matters is enforcing isolation at the lowest possible layer, not by trusting application code. A developer who forgets to add the tenant_id filter to every query by hand will, one day, leak another tenant's data. That's why the filter is embedded in the database policy and the shared query layer; even if a developer wanted to skip it, they couldn't.
In multi-tenant architecture, security is not a feature but the default behavior; not the exception, but the rule.
One core, real-time sync

In Corex360, a message arriving from WhatsApp must appear at the same moment in the inbox, on the CRM card, and on the BI dashboard. We solve this not by constantly polling every screen, but with an event-driven backbone: when something happens — a new message, an issued invoice, a moving vehicle — the system publishes an event, the relevant modules listen for it and update themselves. Even while a single core serves thousands of tenants, each tenant's events travel only within its own boundaries.

This architecture keeps modules loosely coupled. The vehicle tracking module doesn't need to know the inventory module's internal structure; both speak the same event language. Adding a new module means adding a new listener to the stream, not rewriting the core. The secret to scaling is managing not size, but independence.
Per-tenant authorization and KVKK/GDPR

KVKK in Turkey, GDPR in Europe — both demand a clear answer to the question of 'who owns the data and who may see it.' In a multi-tenant system, this means two-layered authorization: first the tenant boundary (a business's user can never access another business's data), then roles within the tenant (the field supervisor sees vehicle data, the accountant sees invoices, not everyone sees everything).

The practical face of compliance is auditability: who accessed which data and when; when a tenant's data is asked to be deleted, that and only that tenant's trace is actually erased; data retention periods can be enforced per tenant. A good multi-tenant architecture carries KVKK not as a compliance layer pasted on afterward, but as a natural consequence of the isolation decision.

saha-ekipleri-arac-takip.title: Bringing the field to the center: vehicle tracking, work orders, and Corex Drive
saha-ekipleri-arac-takip.excerpt: Live vehicle tracking, work order management, and document filing with Corex Drive — bringing field operations under the same roof as sales and finance.

blocks.text: In most businesses, the field is the office's blind spot. Sales lives in the CRM, finance in accounting; but where the vehicle is lives in phone calls, the work order in a WhatsApp group, and the delivery document in a photo on someone's phone. Corex360 takes the field out of being a peripheral department and brings it right into the same command center: vehicle, work order, and document — side by side with sales and finance.
Knowing where the vehicle is means knowing the plan

Live vehicle tracking isn't just a dot on a map. When location, traveled route, and fuel data come together, the real picture of the field emerges: which vehicle is heading to which job, is there a deviation from the planned route, is fuel consumption normal, when will it reach the next delivery. When a customer asks 'When will the team arrive?', instead of guessing, you look at the screen and answer.

Vehicle data is valuable as a dashboard on its own; but its real power emerges when it combines with the work order. The route is no longer an abstract line, but a movement tied to a specific customer and a specific job.
When the field is invisible it can't be managed; once it comes under the same roof, it becomes measurable.
The work order: tracing the job from start to finish

A work order is the field's counterpart to the CRM opportunity. Which customer, which job, which team, which vehicle, in which status — it's tracked in a single record from the moment it opens to the moment it closes. The field team sees the job on their phone, updates the status, marks it done; while the office sees the live status of every work order, the delays, and the completion rate all at once. Coordination scattered across WhatsApp groups gathers into a single stream.

The story doesn't end when the work order closes. That job's invoice is issued in the finance module, the products used are deducted from stock, and it's added to the history on the customer's card. The disconnect between the field and the back office disappears — because both are two faces of the same record.
Corex Drive: keeping the document next to the job

Every field job leaves a document behind: a delivery report, a signed waybill, an installation photo, a warranty form. When these get lost in phone galleries and email attachments, finding proof in a dispute becomes impossible. Corex Drive stores the document right where it belongs — next to the work order, the customer card, and the invoice. For the paperwork you need, you don't open a folder, but the relevant record.

This is the essence of bringing field operations into the same hub as sales and finance: the vehicle, the work order, and the document are no longer pieces of data living in separate systems, but links on the same line of the customer journey. A business's 360° view is completed not where the office window ends, but at the farthest point of the field.
